articles.title: ‘We are not at consensus but we are at convergence’
articles.summary: Ahead of The Longevity Show, Steve Horvath explains why precision measurement is the first step in a preventative health revolution. The Longevity Show returns with a program that reflects a field in transition – no longer content with abstract promises of lifespan extension, but increasingly focused on the practicalities of delivery, measurement and clinical integration. articles.title: GLP-1 drug enters trial for progressive multiple sclerosis
articles.summary: Neuraly-backed study explores whether a long-acting therapy can slow the brain damage behind progressive multiple sclerosis. GLP-1 drugs have been almost impossible to escape since they have dominated conversations about weight loss, celebrity culture, diabetes care and the future of medicine itself. Drugs in the category have been praised, politicized, overhyped and, in some corners […] The post GLP-1 drug enters trial for progressive multiple sclerosis appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Voyager pushes IV gene therapy for Alzheimer’s
articles.summary: Voyager’s latest preclinical results suggest brain-targeted gene therapy may soon move from invasive delivery to simple IV infusion. Alzheimer’s disease has never lacked scientific attention. What it has lacked is delivery. Researchers have been able to identify promising targets in the brain – proteins like tau that appear to drive cognitive decline – but actually […] The post Voyager pushes IV gene therapy for Alzheimer’s appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: SimonMed’s AI imaging expansion targets silent diseases
articles.summary: AI imaging expanded to routine scans, turning everyday exams into tools for early detection and prevention, without extra time or radiation. Diseases that stay quiet are dangerous. No pain, no obvious symptoms; just slow, invisible progression. By the time they manifest themselves, they’ve already taken something: a blocked artery, a fractured bone, a life suddenly […] The post SimonMed’s AI imaging expansion targets silent diseases appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: AI meets ALS in £7.5 million Longitude Prize
articles.summary: Global teams gain funding and data access to identify new drug targets for a complex neurodegenerative disease. A £7.5 million ($10.2m) global challenge prize is placing a deliberate bet on artificial intelligence as a tool to reframe one of medicine’s most intractable problems. The Longitude Prize on ALS has awarded £2 million in “Discovery Awards” […] The post AI meets ALS in £7.5 million Longitude Prize appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Diathrive, KardiaComplete partner to tackle cardiometabolic risk
articles.summary: Diathrive Health and AliveCor’s KardiaComplete announced a strategic partnership to deliver a more comprehensive approach to cardiometabolic care for employers. The collaboration will align Diathrive Health’s diabetes management platform, which provides unlimited testing supplies, personalized guidance from clinically trained health advisors, and real-time data insights, with KardiaComplete’s FDA-cleared medical-grade EKG devices and virtual heart health […] The post Diathrive, KardiaComplete partner to tackle cardiometabolic risk appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Neuvasq presents preclinical data for blood-retina barrier repair
articles.summary: Neuvasq presented preclinical data at the ARVO 2026 meeting showing that multispecific antibodies targeting Gpr124 and Lrp6 support repair of the blood-retina barrier. The most advanced programs are NVQ401, a bispecific antibody that activated Wnt/β-catenin signaling and reversed VEGF-induced vascular permeability in human retinal cells while demonstrating robust efficacy across three preclinical retinopathy models including […] The post Neuvasq presents preclinical data for blood-retina barrier repair appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: AlzeCure publishes preclinical article on ACD137 for osteoarthritis
articles.summary: AlzeCure Pharma published a scientific article in the Scandinavian Journal of Pain reporting preclinical characterization of ACD137, the lead candidate in its TrkA-NAM pain program. The paper, authored by Pontus Forsell, PhD, with multiple co-authors, describes ACD137 as a selective negative allosteric modulator of TrkA that blocks NGF-mediated signaling. According to the company, preclinical data […] The post AlzeCure publishes preclinical article on ACD137 for osteoarthritis appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Executive Health program condenses three months of testing into six hours
articles.summary: Biograph introduced a next-generation Executive Physical that combines whole-body MRI, advanced CT, biomarkers and physician-led analysis to evaluate cardiovascular, cancer, brain and metabolic health in a single day. According to the company, the program condenses more than 30 evaluations into up to six hours compared with what would traditionally take over three months and coordination […] The post Executive Health program condenses three months of testing into six hours appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Researchers develop a biodegradable wash that can remove pesticides and keep fruit fresh longer
articles.summary: Many grocery shoppers know the routine: bring fruit and vegetables home, rinse them, dry them and hope they stay fresh long enough to be eaten. But fresh produce is delicate. Grapes shrivel, apple slices brown and berries can spoil quickly.

articles.title: CRISPR safeguard changes how engineered microbes can be controlled
articles.summary: Engineered microorganisms are widely used in industrial biotechnology and biopharmaceutical applications, including the production of biofuels, sustainable chemicals, and therapeutic compounds. However, concerns remain regarding the unintended environmental release and uncontrolled proliferation of genetically engineered microbes. For this reason, biocontainment technologies, which are designed to prevent microorganisms from surviving outside controlled environments, have become increasingly important in both academia and industry.

articles.title: Bee more specific: New radar tech could improve identification and tracking of key pollinators
articles.summary: Scientists from Trinity and Technical University of Denmark have developed a new radar-based technique that could address a critical gap in global conservation efforts, by transforming how we identify and track the insects that are actually responsible for pollinating plants.

articles.title: AI cuts wildlife tracking time from months to days
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence can dramatically speed up the painstaking work of tracking wildlife with remote cameras, cutting analysis time from months or even a year to just days while producing nearly the same scientific conclusions as humans.

articles.title: AI tool unifies fragmented cell maps into spatial atlases across tissues
articles.summary: A new computational method could dramatically accelerate efforts to map the body's cells in space, according to a study published in Nature Genetics. Spatial multi-omics technologies—often described as ultra-high-resolution maps of tissues—allow scientists to see not only which genes or proteins are active in a cell, but exactly where that activity occurs. That spatial context is critical for understanding complex organs such as the brain, immune tissues and developing embryos.

articles.title: Ultrasound waves rupture COVID-19 and flu viruses without damaging cells
articles.summary: Researchers at the University of São Paulo (USP) in Brazil have discovered that high-frequency ultrasound waves similar to those used in medical exams can eliminate viruses such as SARS-CoV-2 and H1N1 without damaging human cells. In an article published in Scientific Reports, they describe how the phenomenon, known as acoustic resonance, causes structural changes in viral particles until they rupture and become inactivated.

articles.title: Newcomer anti-IL-33 makes strides in COPD
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 07 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03136-x AstraZeneca’s IL-33 antibody tozorakimab targets two distinct signaling pathways engaged in COPD: inflammation and epithelial dysfunction.

articles.title: Membrane complex aids rock-eating microbes in converting carbon dioxide to biomass
articles.summary: So-called rock-eating microorganisms obtain their energy to convert carbon dioxide (CO2) from inorganic sources and make up the vast majority of biomass producers. Using electron microscopy and infrared spectroscopy, a research team from the universities of Potsdam and Marburg has investigated the structure of DAB2 in the sulfur bacterium Halothiobacillus neapolitanus. Their findings have been published in Nature Communications.

articles.title: Death-defying protein found in tardigrades preserves synthetic cells
articles.summary: A protein found only in microscopic tardigrades, one that allows them to survive extreme conditions like dehydration, can convey similar durability in synthetic cells, according to new research from University of Michigan Engineering and the University of Chicago Pritzker School of Molecular Engineering.

articles.title: DNA-guided CRISPR flips gene editing script, opening a new path for precise diagnosis and antivirals
articles.summary: A research team led by Prof. Hsing I-Ming, Professor of the Department of Chemical and Biological Engineering (CBE) at The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (HKUST), in collaboration with Prof. Zhai Yuanliang, Associate Professor of the Division of Life Science (LIFS), has successfully developed the world's first DNA-guided CRISPR-Cas system capable of programmable RNA targeting and cleavage.

articles.title: CZ says crypto exchange rivals opposed his pardon bid
articles.summary: Binance co-founder Changpeng “CZ” Zhao said rival crypto exchanges were concerned a pardon could pave the way for Binance to return to the US market.

articles.title: 7 major Bitcoin mining pools join Stratum V2, working group
articles.summary: Individual miners in Bitcoin mining pools must rely on block templates provided by the mining pool operator.

articles.title: Strategy CEO Phong Le says company will sell BTC only in specific cases
articles.summary: Strategy's Bitcoin sales will not move the markets, despite it owning more than 4% of the digital currency's maximum supply, Le said.

articles.title: Arbitrum’s $71 million in ETH cleared for Aave transfer as North Korea terrorism creditors retain legal claim
articles.summary: The order shields anyone who votes on the transfer from being held in violation of the freeze, though the ultimate fate of the funds remains unclear.

articles.title: BoE’s Bailey warns of looming ‘wrestle’ with US over stablecoin rules, flags run risk for UK
articles.summary: Bailey, who chairs the Financial Stability Board, warned that hard-to-redeem U.S. stablecoins could flood into jurisdictions like the UK during a crisis.

articles.title: The CLARITY Act will help reshore the crypto industry in the US — Attorney
articles.summary: The "biggest market" in crypto is conducting the vast majority of its trading volume outside of US-based exchanges, according to Attorney Bill Hughes.

articles.title: The Nobitex dilemma: How Iran's biggest crypto exchange stays off the OFAC blacklist
articles.summary: Iran entered the final night of February 2026 under a near-total internet shutdown. In the wake of a joint strike by the United States and Israel, Tehran almost completely severed the country's connection to the global internet — likely leaving only users on a government whitelist with access to the outside world.

articles.title: Trump Media’s Q1 loss widens to $406 million on bitcoin, CRO markdowns
articles.summary: The loss was primarily driven by $244 million in unrealized losses on cryptocurrency holdings and an additional $108.2 million investment loss.

articles.title: Sports betting should be regulated as a financial product, not gambling, aspiring prediction market provider says
articles.summary: Novig CEO Jacob Fortinsky said his company will transition to a federal Designated Contract Market framework this summer to launch in all 50 states, while 57 Maiden's Adam Mastrelli said he was banned from two major sportsbooks within two months for being “sharp."

articles.title: LayerZero issues public apology for Kelp DAO exploit response, admits fault in single-verifier setup
articles.summary: LayerZero also disclosed a previously unreported incident in which a multisig signer used their production hardware wallet to execute a personal trade.
